title: Vanilla 8x8
body:
# Vanilla 8x8 

Experience Minecraft the way it was meant to be - as a game made of BLOCKS. This texture pack downscales all vanilla textures to 8x8, creating a bold, retro aesthetic that makes the world feel bigger, the cubes feel cubier, and your builds feel more intentional.

---

## The Illusion

Something magical happens when you lower texture resolution: the world transforms.

**The world feels BIGGER.** With less detail per block, your brain perceives blocks as smaller units in a grander scale. A mountain isn't just tall - it's *massive*. Your castle doesn't just look good - it feels *monumental*. The horizon stretches farther. Every build becomes more epic.

**The cubes feel CUBIER.** When each block face is only 8 pixels wide, the geometry becomes impossible to ignore. Blocks aren't trying to hide what they are - they're celebrating it. The grid structure emerges. Edges sharpen. The world becomes unapologetically blocky, like physical LEGO bricks you can see and feel. This isn't Minecraft pretending to be realistic - this is Minecraft being purely, beautifully itself.

**Detail becomes intentional.** Every pixel matters. Every block placement counts. You're not painting with textures - you're building with blocks. The simplicity forces creativity. The limitations become the aesthetic.

---
![A Blocky Forest. ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c3667992ebb999f6a87f683c18017166f90026ae_0.webp)
---

## The Retro Feel

Remember when games were made of pixels, not polygons trying to be photographs? This pack brings back that classic video game aesthetic:

- **NES/SNES era charm** - Bold pixels, clear shapes, pure style
- **Arcade cabinet vibes** - Bright, readable, iconic
- **Game Boy nostalgia** - Simple but expressive
- **Early Minecraft alpha** - Before HD packs, before shaders, just blocks

This is Minecraft stripped down to its core idea: a world made of cubes. Just pure blocks.

---

## Why 8x8?

**Performance:**
- 4x less VRAM than vanilla 16x16
- Faster loading times
- Smoother gameplay on any hardware
- Perfect for integrated graphics or older PCs
- +20-40% FPS boost on low-end systems

**Aesthetic:**
- Maximum blockiness
- Clear, readable textures
- Strong geometric shapes
- Pixels you can actually see and appreciate
- That chunky retro game look

**Experience:**
- World feels more expansive
- Builds feel more impressive
- Embraces Minecraft's blocky nature
- Less visual noise, more clarity
- Focus on shape and form over texture detail

---

## What Changed

Every texture is downscaled from vanilla 16x16 to 8x8 using nearest-neighbor interpolation (no blur, crisp pixels):

- **Blocks** - All block textures (stone, wood, grass, etc.)
- **Items** - All item textures
- **Entities** - Mobs, players, armor
- **GUI** - Menus, buttons, icons
- **Environment** - Particles, sky, effects

Everything is exactly half the resolution of vanilla, creating a consistent retro aesthetic throughout the entire game.

---

## Perfect For

- Low-end PCs and laptops
- Players who want better performance
- Retro gaming enthusiasts
- Builders who want epic scale
- Anyone tired of overly detailed HD packs
- Classic Minecraft purists
- People who think blocks should look like blocks

---

## Compatibility

### Requirements
- **Minecraft Version:** 1.21.1 (works with 1.21.x)
- **No mods required** - Works in vanilla Minecraft
- **Optifine compatible** - Works great with performance mods

### Works With
- Vanilla Minecraft
- Fabric/Forge
- Most shaders (though shaders kind of miss the point)
- Other resource packs (if they don't override textures)
- Multiplayer servers (client-side only)

### May Conflict With
- Other texture packs that modify vanilla textures
- HD texture packs (completely opposite aesthetic)

**Recommended:** Use with classic lighting and no shaders for maximum retro effect.

---

**Go retro. Go blocky. Go big.**

Experience Minecraft as a game of blocks again.
